data.title: Brand Engagement Network to acquire Cataneo in $19.5M deal
data.description:
• Brand Engagement Network (NASDAQ: BNAI) on Thursday said it has signed a definitive agreement to acquire Cataneo GmbH in a $19.5 million debt-free transaction.
  • The deal will be funded through a mix of cash and stock, with about $10.5 million to be paid in equity and the remainder in cash supported by committed financing.
  • Cataneo provides media and advertising infrastructure software used to manage large-scale advertising inventory across broadcasters and digital platforms.
  • The companies said the combination will integrate Brand Engagement Network’s conversational AI technology with Cataneo’s media systems to enable more automated and personalized advertising engagement.
  • The transaction is expected to close by June 30, 2026, subject to customary conditions.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Deutsche Post AG GAAP EPS of €0.73, revenue of €20.42B

  • Revenue of  €20.42B (-1.9% Y/Y).

data.title: Jones Soda to raise about $2.5M in private placement
data.description:
• Jones Soda Co. (CSE: JSDA, OTCQB: JSDA) on Thursday said it has agreed to a brokered private placement to raise up to about $2.5 million in gross proceeds.
  • The company will issue up to 7.5 million units at $0.33 per unit, with each unit consisting of one common share and half of a warrant.
  • The offering is expected to close around May 7, 2026, subject to customary conditions.
  • Each full warrant will allow the purchase of one additional share at $0.45 for 36 months following closing.
  • The company may accelerate warrant expiration if its share price exceeds $0.47 for five consecutive trading days.
  • Proceeds will be used for general working capital.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: U.S. Q1 GDP rises 2.0%, less than expected in initial print; prices rise more
data.description:
U.S. GDP  rose +2.0% Q/Q, on an annualized rate, in Q1 2026, less than the +2.2% consensus, but a marked increase from the 0.5% growth in Q4, according to advance data released by the Bureau of Economic Analysis on Thursday. Inflation resurged, according to the data, as oil prices surged with the onset of the Iran war.

As inflation increased, consumer spending growth slowed. The Q1 increase in GDP also contrasts with Q4, when the government shutdown weighed on economic growth.

Contributors to Q1's GDP growth were investment, exports, consumer spending, and government spending. Imports, which are subtracted from GDP, also rose. The consumer spending increase primarily reflected an increase in services, led by health care. The rise in investment reflected increases in equipment, intellectual property products, and private inventory investment that were partly offset by decreases in residential and nonresidential structures, the BEA said.

The personal consumption expenditures, or PCE, price index grew +4.5%, more than the +4.1% consensus and accelerating from +2.9% in Q4..

Core PCE prices (excluding food and energy) increased 4.3% vs. +4.1% consensus and 2.7% prior.

Personal consumption expenditures rose 1.6% in Q1 2026 vs. 1.9% in Q4.

Real final sales to domestic purchasers: +2.5% vs. +1.8% in prior.

Developing… Check back for updates.

data.title: Air France-KLM SA reports Q1 results
data.description:
• Air France-KLM SA press release (AFLYY): Q1
  • Revenue of €7.5B (+4.5% Y/Y).
  • Unit revenue 1 was up 3.4% supported by ongoing premiumization and by reduced industry capacity due to the Middle East conflict, while group capacity grew by 4.0%.
  • Unit cost increased 0.5% reflecting disciplined cost management, productivity gains and fleet renewal, despite the increase in premiumization.
  • Operating result at -€27m, up €301m compared to last year.
  • 2026 OUTLOOK REVISED GIVEN CURRENT GEOPOLITICAL UNCERTAINTY FY 2026
  • Group Capacity +2-4% vs. 2025 Previously “+3-5%” 0% to +2%
  • Unit cost1 Including 0.5% increase linked to cabin premiumization (unchanged)
  • Net Capex (c.80% fleet/fleet related) Below €3bn Previously c.€3bn
  • Net Debt/Current EBITDA 1.5x to 2.0x Unchanged
